summary: Mellow first set, high‑energy third, standout acoustic & China Doll
blurb: The show opens with a relaxed first set highlighted by “Oh Babe, It Ain't No Lie” and “Terrapin Station”. The second set delivers “Feel Like A Stranger”, “Mexicali Blues”, and “Tennessee Jed”, while the third set builds to an uplifting finale featuring “The Music Never Stopped” and “Samson & Delilah”. Overall performance is strong with Jerry’s subtle vocals and solid drumming, though some audience noise can distract on certain recordings.
review: On October 23‑1980 at Radio City Music Hall the Dead served a deliberately laid‑back first set that let the acoustic vibe breathe. Jerry Garcia kept his voice low‑key on “China Doll” and slipped in tasteful licks on “Mexicali Blues”, while Brent’s keyboards added texture without overwhelming the mix. The second set lifted the energy with “Feel Like A Stranger”, “Tennessee Jed”, and a rousing “Big Railroad Blues”. By the third set the band was fully engaged, driving “The Music Never Stopped” with both Mickey and Billy hammering out a tight groove that carried into an uplifting finish with “Samson & Delilah” and a heartfelt encore of “Casey Jones”. The show shines on several recordings, especially the superb soundboard capture that presents crystal‑clear instrument separation and captures the night’s mellow yet dynamic spirit.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Strong positive sentiment across multiple recordings: three reviews describe the performance as excellent or great, highlighting many standout songs and solid playing; only one negative note concerns audience noise, not musical quality. Mixed sentiments are limited to a couple of sources, so overall sentiment is clearly favorable.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crisp front‑of‑house mix, captures mellow first set and standout tracks in the cleanest audio of the night.

key_highlights: Acoustic opening is essentially perfect, delivering intimate harmonies.
Second set shines with “Feel Like A Stranger”, “Mexicali Blues” and “Tennessee Jed”.
Third set builds to an uplifting finale featuring “The Music Never Stopped” and “Samson & Delilah”.
Soundboard capture offers crystal‑clear mix, making this the most enjoyable listening version.
song_highlights: Oh Babe, It Ain't No Lie

band_performance.Jerry: Delivered a relaxed, melodic performance; his vocal shines on “China Doll” and he adds inventive licks on “Mexicali Blues”, while keeping the first set deliberately mellow.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides steady, restrained pulse in the mellow first set and drives an energetic groove during “The Music Never Stopped”.
band_performance.Billy: Pairs with Mickey to keep a solid rhythmic foundation; prominent on the opening of “On The Road Again” and fuels the high‑energy moments later in the show.

must_listen_sequences: Oh Babe, It Ain't No Lie
